title: Connect a Google Spreadsheet with Monday Boards using a CSV file URL - Free App!

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Make sure you post answers the following questions so users know exactly what the value is:

- What is your app called?
Monday CSV Converter for Google Sheets

- Why did you build this app?
I needed to connect a Google Sheet with a Monday board so I wouldn’t have to keep updating the both sides.

- What does your app do?
It’ll give you a URL you can copy and paste in you Google Sheet, it’ll pull all the current data from the board.

- What example use cases and workflows does this app apply to? Be specific.
You have a Google Sheet you use for the same report/graphs, and you want to connect it to a Monday board so it’ll fetch the changes every time you open the file.

- Who are you? Are you a monday partner?
My name is Yogev, and I don’t think I’m considered a Monday Partner

- How can users use your app?
Just go to this address: Monday CSV
Log in with your Monday account so it’ll install the app.
Follow the instructions, I added a screenshot showing a CSV URL.

- Are there any dependencies (eg: must have an enterprise subscription to X tool)
No

- Does it have a cost?
No

Here’s how a new CSV URL looks like, you can add as many as you’d like, they will remain available as long as the board exists on Monday and you didn’t delete the URL from the app.

I hope you’ll enjoy this app

image1485×913 58.7 KB

posts.cookedHtml: Hi Yoggie, that is exactly the solution I am looking for. Can you reactivate the app somehow?

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @metalsludge
Not sure what the problem is, try reinstalling it here:

auth.monday.com

Welcome to monday.com | a new way of working

monday.com Work OS is an open platform where anyone can create the tools they need to run every aspect of their work.

posts.author: Alchemist (David @ Done.Works)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a really cool app.
Is there any way to automate the refresh of the google sheet without having to open the file?

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Alchemist
Yes of course.
On your google sheet go to extensions->apps scripts

Paste this template and change the relevant variables:

function myFunction() {
  SpreadsheetApp.getActive().getRange('A1').setValue('=importData("https://twodo-csv-be.herokuapp.com/api/monday/convertcsv/XXXXXXXXXXXXX")')
}

Go yo current project triggers and set up the frequency of the refresh - please don’t make it too frequent as it might lock the integration (it’s a free up so I had to add some rules so it won’t crush).

Hope this helps!

posts.cookedHtml:

One question - what’s the best way to handle exporting boards that have subitems or have connected board / mirrored columns? Since this requires 2 (or more) links, how do you import these into excel?

posts.author: Yoggie (Yogev David)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @rckent!
So glad you found it useful!

Try the MondayReport app:

Export to Excel (CSV) Platform discussions

It might solve your problem.

posts.author: Kelliw (Kelli Witham)
posts.cookedHtml:
I want to pull in the time tracking information (date/time/person) from subtasks. We are able to individually pull each report to excel from a main task. Will this URL you created let me pull each into an excel template?
When I pull the full board it does not pull the information of who/date of the time tracking added in subtasks. It only pulls to excel when I select a task and in the subtask column of time tracking pull those to excel… hence the individual pulling issue.

Could there be a security setting in Google Sheets preventing handover?
